settings.chunk_size_hint: Large files are split into chunks. Smaller = more reliable, larger = faster.
settings.concurrency_hint: Chunks uploaded in parallel. Higher = faster (depends on your connection).

files.drop_subtitle: Files will be uploaded to the current folder

viewer.video_unsupported_title: Your browser can't play this format directly
viewer.video_unsupported_desc: {ext} needs an external codec. Download and open it in a desktop media player.

trash.title: Trash
trash.retention_hint: Deleted files are kept for 14 days and can be restored. They still count toward your quota.
trash.confirm_purge: Permanently delete? This cannot be undone.
trash.confirm_empty: Empty trash? All items will be permanently deleted.

modal.delete_warning: Items move to Trash and can be restored within 14 days. They still count toward your quota.

share.no_shares_hint: Right-click a file → "Share" to create one

shared_page.not_found: Share link not found or expired

help.title: NkCloud Help
help.browse_desc:
Click a folder to enter it. Use the breadcrumb bar at the top to jump back.
Toggle between list and grid view. Sort by name, size, or date modified.
help.select_desc:
Click checkbox — Toggle single item
Ctrl + Click (Mac: ⌘ + Click) — Toggle multiple
Shift + Click — Select range
Ctrl + A (Mac: ⌘ + A) — Select all / deselect all
Esc — Clear selection
A toolbar appears when items are selected for batch download, move, delete.
help.text_desc:
Plain text, Markdown, code, logs, JSON, YAML and similar formats open in-browser with line numbers and encoding info.
Limit: 5 MB. Larger files: download and open locally.
help.trash_desc:
Deleted files move to Trash and are kept for 14 days — restore anytime during that window.
Trash still counts toward your quota. Use Delete forever or Empty trash to reclaim space.
Expired items are purged automatically when you next open Trash.
help.upload_desc:
Click the Upload button or drag & drop files or folders onto the page.
Large files (> 20MB) are automatically chunked with progress display.
Dropping a folder preserves the subdirectory structure — matching folders are created automatically.
help.download_desc:
Click a file to download (media files preview first).
Right-click a folder to Download as ZIP.
help.media_desc:
Images, videos, and audio open in a preview viewer.
← → keys to navigate, Esc or click the background to close.
Images: scroll wheel to zoom (toward pointer), drag to pan, double-click to toggle 100% / fit.
help.share_desc:
Right-click a file or folder → Share. Options:
• Password protection
• Expiry time
The generated link can be opened by anyone (no login required).
help.context_desc:
Right-click any file or folder for quick actions:
Open, download, preview, rename, share, move, delete.
help.webdav_desc:
You can also mount NkCloud as a network drive via WebDAV.
macOS Finder: Go → Connect to Server → Enter WebDAV URL
Credentials: Your NkCloud username and password
help.shortcut_select_all: Ctrl + A (Mac: ⌘ + A) — Select all / Deselect all
help.shortcut_home_end: Home / End — Jump to first / last item
help.role_owner_desc:
As Owner, you have full control:
• Browse and manage all files (root + all user homes)
• Upload, delete, move any file
• Create and revoke invite links
• Manage users (promote/demote, set quotas, disable/delete)
• View audit logs of all operations
• Create and manage share links
help.role_admin_desc:
As Admin, you can:
• Browse all files (root + all user homes, root is read-only)
• Upload and manage files within user home directories
• Delete other users' files (but not the owner's)
• Create invite links for new users
• Create and manage share links

You cannot: upload to root, delete owner's files, manage users, or change quotas.
help.role_user_desc:
As a Member, you can:
• Browse, upload, download, and delete files in your own directory
• Create share links for your files
• Search within your files
• Use WebDAV to access your directory

Your files are stored in a private home directory that only you and admins can see.
